newsSinceJun6: No verified company-specific news since June 6. The +9.0% June 9 move appears flow-driven: sympathy with US semiconductor/Intel strength and continued AI-substrate momentum chasing, not a new fundamental catalyst. The last material datapoints predate the window: record FY results reported May 11 (record net profit ~JPY 63.7B, electronics segment OP +70% YoY on AI demand) and the February 2026 ~JPY 500B IC package substrate capacity expansion plan (FY2026-28, Gama/Ono plants).

sources.label: Investing.com - Ibiden raises forecast on AI server demand, plans capacity expansion
Ibiden notice - capital investment plan for high-performance IC package substrates (Feb 2026)
Moomoo Tokyo Market Open (Apr 24, ruled out as today's driver - Intel Q2 guide sympathy rally)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a crowding-sensitive residual-alpha list, a +9% single-day melt-up with no identifiable new fundamental evidence, stacked on +160% YTD and a $32B cap, further compresses what little hidden/under-owned upside remained; this is new price action beyond yesterday's down call.
verdicts.bull: Ibiden remains a genuine bottleneck: high-end ABF/IC package substrates for Intel and Nvidia AI servers, with a JPY 500B capacity plan that locks in multi-year volume if AI server demand holds.
verdicts.neutral: The thesis is intact but fully discovered; record May earnings and the capex roadmap are in the price, so the name is now a benchmark/monitor proxy rather than an alpha source.
verdicts.bear: A flow-driven +9% day on no news is classic late-stage chasing; any AI-server digestion, Intel demand wobble, or substrate capacity ramp by Korean/Taiwanese rivals into 2027 hits a stock priced for perfection.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY2026 results and substrate pricing/utilization commentary; first evidence on Gama/Ono expansion ramp timing.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: momentum reversal in a fully rerated, index-owned name with no remaining valuation cushion.

verdicts.rankRationale: Within the advanced packaging & substrates bucket the scarcity exposure is real, but this page's alpha score already balanced bottleneck quality against rerating; today's news-free +9% pop extends the rerating without adding new fundamental evidence, so residual score should bleed lower again.
verdicts.bull: Substrate scarcity for AI server CPUs/GPUs is structural through 2027, and Ibiden is the quality leader with committed capex and record AI-driven segment profits backing the thesis.
verdicts.neutral: Execution and demand are tracking the thesis, but the stock has moved faster than the fundamentals since the May print; hold the bucket exposure, trim the score.
verdicts.bear: 159% YTD plus a 9% no-news day means the packaging-scarcity story is consensus; cyclicality and 2027-28 industry capacity adds could turn scarcity pricing into oversupply just as new plants ramp.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Substrate supply/demand updates from Intel and Nvidia server roadmaps; capacity ramp milestones at Gama and Ono plants.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating valuation compression if AI server substrate tightness eases or order momentum decelerates.
